summary: Backend smoke + core auth/links/CPI tests for RealFlow. 18/18 pytest cases passed against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Verified: /api/diagnostics/health (200), /api/admin/login (admin@realflow.local/admin123 → token, is_admin=true), /api/auth/register, /api/auth/login (+ invalid creds returns 401), /api/links (403 for pending user → 200 [] after admin activation, full create→get→delete cycle works), /api/cpi/offers (200 after enabling `cpi` feature). No 5xx on any core path.

minor.issue: Passlib emits non-fatal warning `module 'bcrypt' has no attribute '__about__'` due to passlib 1.7.x not knowing bcrypt 4.x. Password hashing/verification still works (proven by login tests). Cosmetic only — pin bcrypt<4 or upgrade to passlib >=1.7.5 fork to silence.

action_items: (Optional) Silence passlib/bcrypt startup warning by pinning bcrypt<4 in backend/requirements.txt or upgrading passlib.

(Design note) Newly-registered users start with status=pending and ALL feature flags False — this is intentional but means a fresh signup cannot use /api/links or /api/cpi/* until admin activates them. Confirm this is the intended onboarding UX.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~13,626 lines — strongly recommend splitting per-domain (auth, admin, links, clicks, cpi already split, rut, gsheet, email, diagnostics). Maintenance and review at this size is dangerous.

DEFAULT_FEATURES has every flag False (including `links`), but the registration flow does not surface to the new user that they're locked out until admin approval. A clearer 403 message or onboarding state would help. Currently the 403 detail does mention `Contact admin at {ADMIN_CONTACT_EMAIL}` which is good.
MongoDB ObjectId is correctly excluded via {"_id": 0} projection across the routes inspected — good.

Admin /api/admin/users/{id} update accepts arbitrary feature keys via `extra='allow'` on UserFeatures — safe because gated by admin auth, but worth documenting which keys are recognized (e.g., `cpi` is not in the default UserFeatures schema yet works because of extra=allow).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@realflow.local / admin123 (from /app/backend/.env). Test users are created/destroyed per run with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Each run registers one TEST_<uuid>@realflow.local user, activates them via admin, creates+deletes one link. Session teardown deletes any leftover TEST_ users via /api/admin/users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_realflow_core.py is the canonical regression set for core backend smoke. It exercises register→login→admin-activate→links CRUD→cpi list and self-cleans via a session teardown fixture. The /api/links and /api/cpi/* endpoints REQUIRE the admin to (a) set status=active and (b) flip the feature flag on the user — fresh signups will 403 by design. CPI feature key is literally `cpi` (cpi_module.py:220). DB name is `realflow`. Backend exposes via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://dynabook-dev.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: No bug. All targeted endpoints behave correctly. The single non-fatal warning is passlib<>bcrypt4 metadata mismatch and has no functional impact.
